title: Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire

sections.title: Inspecting the Ancient Vault after starting the quest

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: I don't understand... How?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well, kid, any thoughts on my offer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking around. I'll decide once I know a bit more about what this place is.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Of course! Take as much time as you need, as long as you don't take long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I know you! You're that archaeologist! What was your name again...?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: You don't remember my name?! You don't remember the world famous Asgarnia Smith, archaeologist extraordinaire?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... sorry. It's not a very memorable name.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: All that work we did together, and you don't even remember my name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be fair, we never really did that much work together. You sent me on a treasure hunt, but you never really contributed much yourself.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: And a good job I didn't! I never saw a single ounce of gold from that partnership.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well if a partnership with me was so disappointing, how come you're here?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Bah, I'm not here for you, kid. I'm here for treasure!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: After our last escapade turned out to be pointless, I set out in search of a new adventure! I started off on Karamja, but that didn't really go anywhere...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: In the end, I came back to the desert and dug up this place! I've spent ages trying to work out how to get in, only for you to walk up and open the door right away!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... funny that. They opened for me because of some magical abilities I gained on our last treasure hunt. You know, that one you were saying was pointless.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Huh... Well, who'd have thought...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Alright then, kid, I'll tell you what... You didn't find us any proper treasure last time around, so now's your chance to redeem yourself!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: I'm sure this place is the key to finding riches beyond imagination, and I, Asgarnia Smith, am willing to enter into a partnership with you, er...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't remember my name either, do you? It's [player name].

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Yes, as I was saying, I, Asgarnia Smith, am willing to enter into a partnership with you, [player name], to uncover those riches!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Who knows, perhaps you'll even learn a thing or two from a master treasure hun- I mean, a master archaeologist like me.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: And I am willing to offer you an exclusive rate of a forty percent split!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fifty percent.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Absolutely! A forty-five percent split! So, kid, what do you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about I look around while I think about it?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Of course!

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia Smith after inspecting the vault

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Good to see you, kid. Did you speak to Terry Balando?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know I still haven't agreed to help you, right?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: You'll find Terry at the Digsite Exam Centre. You'd best get going. There's treasure to uncover!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh*

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well, kid, any thoughts on my offer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you not want to hear about what I've found first?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Oh, yes, go ahead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: According to that plaque over there, this place is a vault that was built to protect the 'mighty treasures' of the 'Empty Lord' from 'the soulless betrayers'.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Mighty treasures, you say? Fantastic! Any idea how to access them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm guessing it has something to do with these statues. It looks like each of them is meant to be holding something.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Ah, so we need to find whatever it is they should be holding. Classic treasure vault puzzle! Where do you plan to start?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well hang on there. I haven't actually agreed to anything yet.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: But surely you can't turn down an opportunity like this! You said it yourself: Mighty treasures!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mighty treasures that we can't access.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Now look here, kid. You won't get far in life with an attitude like that. Is there really nothing else we have to go on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We have four names: Vardorvis, Perseriya, Duke Sucellus and the Whisperer.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: They sound like pretty solid leads to me. If we can find these four individuals, maybe we can also find whatever it is we need to get to the treasure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a big 'if' right there. This vault is clearly Zarosian, which means whoever these four are, they're from thousands of years ago. They're probably all long- dead now.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Zarosian? I'm sure I've heard that term before...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Ah yes! Those recent discoveries at the Digsite! I bet my good friend Terry Balando can help. Why don't you talk to him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Again, I've not actually agreed to help.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: No, but you're going to. I can tell. The thrill of the adventure is too much to resist. Not to mention that thirty percent split!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fifty percent.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: So we're agreed? Excellent! You'll find Terry at the Digsite Exam Centre. Good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh*

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Hello again. Did you speak to Dr Banikan?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: He's set up a new dig on the eastern edge of the Digsite. You'll likely find him there.

sections.title: Talking to Terry Balando again after talking to Dr Banikan

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Hello again. How goes the archaeology?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty good, thanks.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Glad to hear it!

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Hello again. How goes the archaeology?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pretty good, thanks. In fact, I've come across something I was hoping you could help me with.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I came across something very interesting down in the desert: A Zarosian vault.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Zarosian? Like that artefact you found for us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly! I'm hoping to learn about four names I discovered on statues within the vault.

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Sounds like an exciting discovery, but I'm afraid I can't help. This 'Zaros' is still mostly a mystery to me. In fact, I'd say you probably know more than I do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what would you suggest?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Well you may well be in luck. We had a new arrival at the Digsite recently who claims to be a Zarosian expert!

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Quite impressive for one of those to exist considering the greatest historians and archaeologists in the world has no knowledge on Zaros until very recently...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... Where can I find this expert?

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: He's set up a new dig on the eastern edge of the Digsite. You're looking for... Ah, what was his name again...

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Nabanik? No, that's not right...

lines.speaker: Terry Balando
lines.text: Banikan! You'll be wanting to speak to Dr Banikan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! I'll head over there and see if I can find him.

sections.title: Attempting to visit the new dig before speaking to Terry Balando
Talking to Dr Banikan again before agreeing to help

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Good day, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what is it you're searching for?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: You seem to be fairly knowledgeable when it comes to the Zarosian Empire, which is quite a rarity these days. How much do you know about its fall?

sections.title: Talking to Dr Banikan again after agreeing to help

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do again?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Grab yourself a pickaxe and carefully clear away these rocks. There should be a pickaxe in the crate in the corner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Oh... Good day, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dr Banikan, I presume?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Banikan? Yes... I am Banikan.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? You don't seem very sure...

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: My apologies. I was not expecting company down here, so you caught me a bit off guard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... We haven't met before, have we? You seem familiar.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I don't believe so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: So tell me, adventurer, what brings you down here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been told you're a Zarosian expert. I was hoping you could help me with some names.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Names? What names?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Vardorvis, Perseriya, Duke Sucellus and the Whisperer. I believe they were all Zarosians.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Where did you hear these names?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a strange vault down in the desert, on the eastern coast. The names were on four statues inside.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: A Zarosian vault? How curious...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can you help?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I think so. I suspect the information you seek can be found in the very place I'm searching for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excellent! Where's that?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: You seem to be fairly knowledgeable when it comes to the Zarosian Empire, which is quite a rarity these days. How much do you know about its fall?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know that Zaros was overthrown by one of his most trusted generals, Zamorak, who became a god as a result.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Spot on! You could teach the so-called experts around here a thing or two. The betrayal of Zaros ultimately led to the God Wars, making it quite possibly the most pivotal moment in all of history.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: However, even to those of us who do know our stuff, much of what happened to the empire following Zaros' defeat is still a mystery. That's why I'm here.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Senntisten, the Zarosian settlement once located here, was believed to be the capital of the empire.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: If my research is right, and I have strong reason to believe it is, the remains of a key military installation are located right beneath us.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I can definitely think of worse places to discover details on the fall of the empire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you think this military installation could have information on the four names I found?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Absolutely! Any respectable military outpost would have been filled to the brim with key strategic intelligence. I'd expect details on notable Zarosians to be included.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Of course, they wouldn't have wanted that information falling into the wrong hands, so recovering it may not be straightforward...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I do love a good challenge.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Glad to hear it. Now, I have made good progress in excavating these ruins so far, but perhaps you can help me with the final step?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like something I'd be good at! What do you need me to blow up?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Excuse me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, my first major discovery here involved me blowing a big hole in some rocks to access an ancient temple. Shouldn't be too hard to do that again.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I'm sorry, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's the problem?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: You risked the structural integrity of an ancient temple by blowing it open with explosives?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh... I guess I never thought about it like that before. Yeah... now that you mention it, that was really quite a stupid idea.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: How about we take just a touch more care with this one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good idea. What do you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Grab yourself a pickaxe and carefully clear away these rocks. There should be a pickaxe in the crate in the corner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, will do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how come you know so much about Zaros?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Well... in my travels, I ended up spending a great deal of time in one of the few places Zarosian remnants can still be found. My time studying them filled me with fascination, and sadness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sadness?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: 'I walked across the sands and saw the ruins of time...'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: It's an old poem about the rise, and inevitable fall, of kingdoms and empires. Once, Zaros' Empire stretched as far as the eye could see. Now, no one even remembers his name.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: To think that something that was once so absolute may soon be lost forever... It falls to people like me, and perhaps you as well, to ensure that history isn't forgotten.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it you're searching for here?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: As I said, I hope to find details about the fall of the Zarosian Empire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything in particular though?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I have a few leads. Details on individuals and artefacts that may have played a key role in the final days of the Empire.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think my four names could be connected?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I'd say it's definitely possible. I suppose there's only one way to find out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd best get to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the rocks cleared.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Excellent work. Shall we head in?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go in first and make sure it's safe. I'll call you down when you're good to join me.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I appreciate your concern for my safety. Very well, I will await your call.

sections.title: Talking to Dr Banikan after clearing rocks
Talking to Dr Banikan before defeating the Ancient Guardian

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Is it safe for me to head below?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll call you down when you're good to join me.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Excellent work. Shall we head in?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: This place is remarkable! Let's have a look around and see what we can find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, you're good to come down now.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: So what was all that noise I heard?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was some sort of ancient golem. I'm guessing it was here to guard the place. Don't worry, I dealt with it.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may have destroyed it...

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: What is it with you and destroying things?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's kind of what I do.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Well let's try and keep the destruction to a minimum from now on. If we're to discover anything down here, we'll need some things still intact.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: And on that note, we should take a look around.

sections.title: Using uncharged cells on the golem machine
Using charged cells on the golem machine

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look at this.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: A golem? Fascinating... Is this like the one that was defending this place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's very similar. Doesn't look like this one will be doing much fighting though.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: No. It seems to have been heavily modified in some way. I wonder...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you thinking?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Golems do have rudimentary intelligence. Maybe we can get some information out of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll need to power it up I guess. Let me see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... This altar could be of interest.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: It may be of little use in its current state. It doesn't seem to be giving off any magical energy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wouldn't have expected an archaeologist to be a magical expert. Do you practice magic much?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I've dabbled.

sections.title: Talking to Dr Banikan before powering up the golem machine

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: We need to find a way to get that golem working.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Let's see what we can find.

sections.title: Failing to power up the golem machine

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ouchie!

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Shocking!

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Careful now!

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: That looked like it hurt.

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Please specify a search term.

sections.title: Talking to Dr Banikan before finishing searching for all of the names

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Need any help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any thoughts on Vardorvis?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: It mentioned Lovakengj, presumably as in the Great Kourend Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't imagine there's many others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any thoughts on Duke Sucellus?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: It mentioned this Duke taking informal command. Seems he was quite important.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And unless the Zarosians had two prisons of the same name, I think I know where the entry to this one is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any thoughts on the Whisperer?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: It mentioned Lassar, one of the old Zarosian fortresses, built atop Ice Mountain.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: It was supposedly defined by its beautiful shining spires...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's keep searching.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Azzanadra?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Mahjarrat. I encountered him in the Kharidian Desert.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Not many people can say they've met a Mahjarrat. Some don't even believe they exist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've actually come across a fair few.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Really? How curious...

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Do you wish to specify another search term?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I think that's all the information I needed.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: And quite the glut of information it was. So I suppose you intend to try and discover what happened to these four individuals?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think that's a good idea?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Absolutely! You have an opportunity here to uncover some lost history of the Zarosian Empire. You can't say no to that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'll be sure to let you know if I find anything interesting.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I'd appreciate that. Anyway, time to see if this golem has what I'm after as well...

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Find what you wanted?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Yes and no. It's not the definitive information I was hoping for, but at least it rules out some options.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I need to consult with a good friend of mine on this. Forgive me, but he lives quite far from here, so best I don't delay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Thank you for your help, adventurer. I hope we meet again soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder what it was that he was searching for...

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Please specify a search term.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Repeat last search.

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Checking...

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Data found. Outputting now.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Lassar... One of the old Zarosian fortresses, built atop Ice Mountain.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: It was supposedly defined by its beautiful shining spires...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well those spires are long gone, but I wonder if there might be some traces left below...

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Camdozaal. This golem here looks suspiciously like the ones I've seen there.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Ah, of course. We know that Saradominists built a new city over the top of Senntisten. It's quite possible the dwarves did the same at Lassar.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: A few of the other archaeologists recently set up a dig in Camdozaal. Perhaps they'll have found something of note.

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Do you wish to specify another search term?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Lovakengj? As in the Great Kourend Lovakengj?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't imagine there's many others.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I've never been myself, but I do remember one of the other archaeologists, Elissa I think is her name, saying she'd visited Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suspect she probably didn't encounter this Vardorvis while there.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: No, I imagine not. Still, from what she was saying, it sounded like she did spend time researching the history of the local area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose there's worse places to start.

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Do you wish to specify another search term?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Battle for the Eye? I've never heard of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I know what it's referring to. There was a great battle at the Temple of the Eye long ago. A rift to the Abyss was opened as a result.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: The Abyss? I've only ever heard legends of the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did the legends say?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Nothing good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... sounds about right.

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Do you wish to specify another search term?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Informal command? Seems this Duke was quite important.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And unless the Zarosians had two prisons of the same name, I think I know where the entry to this one is.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. It's in a dungeon under Weiss. I had quite the adventure there not too long ago.

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Fascinating...

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Do you wish to specify another search term?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it's working!

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Looks like it. Excellent work.

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Please specify a search term.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... What?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I guess you should do as it asks.

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Please specify a search term.

lines.text: Checking...

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Error: No data found.

lines.text: Please specify another search term.

lines.text: Error: Access to data denied.

lines.text: Please specify another search term.

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Data found. Outputting now.

lines.text: Do you wish to specify another search term?

lines.speaker: Golem
lines.text: Please specify a search term.

sections.title: Speaking to Dr Banikan again before he decides search the golem

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I think that's all the information I needed.

sections.title: Speaking to Dr Banikan again after he decides search the golem

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: Let's see if this golem has what I'm after as well.

sections.title: Operating the golem to find out what Dr Banikan searched

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder what it was that he was searching for...

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you visited Lovakengj recently.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: That's right. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm trying to learn more about a vampyre called Vardorvis. Apparently he travelled to the west long ago in pursuit of someone called Yonrith Lovakengj.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: A vampyre? This must have been a very long time ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Back when Senntisten still stood, it seems.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Oh wow, that is a long time! You're talking about a time before Great Kourend even existed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed. I was wondering if during your travels there, you came across any details about Vardorvis or Yonrith?

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Hmm... You say this vampyre was in pursuit of Yonrith?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: Well... it could be nothing, but there's this monument in Lovakengj called the Burning Man. Apparently, it represents the defeat of an ancient evil. Could be a good place to start?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Definitely sounds worth a look.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: You'll find it in western Lovakengj. It's pretty hard to miss!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you visited Lovakengj recently.

lines.speaker: Elissa
lines.text: We've had this conversation before, haven't we? If I were you, I'd check out the Burning Man monument in western Lovakengj. Apparently it represents the defeat of an ancient evil.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Thanks for the help.

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: Hello again!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello! Where did you say that botanist lived?

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: If I remember right, he lived just south of the Hosidius Estate Agent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect! Thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: Don't mention it!

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: Hello! Here to see the Burning Man?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: Well you picked a great day for it! The name's Barus by the way. I work as a historian here. Those Arceuus lot may think they know it all, but I reckon I could tell you a thing or two that even they haven't realised!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well on that note, what can you tell me about the Burning Man?

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: The Burning Man is a monument to a great triumph in Lovakengj history. We're talking thousands of years ago, before Kourend was even a kingdom! Sadly, that does mean some details have been lost to time.

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: What we do know is that a great evil threatened the earliest dwarven settlers here. Had that evil prevailed, Lovakengj might never have existed!

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: Thankfully, with guidance from their brave leader, the settlers were able to overcome this evil! The Burning Man is a reminder of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I'm guessing the monument represents this evil?

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: That's right!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what was it, and where did it come from?

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: Ah, now that bit is less clear. Have you heard of the Stranglewood?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: Not too surprising. It's not a place you'd want to visit. Anyway, that's supposedly where this evil emerged from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. What can you tell me about the Stranglewood?

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: You'll find it on the border between the Kebos Lowlands and the Kingdom of Varlamore. It's a cursed place. Few have ever entered, and of those that have, none have returned!

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: You know, I heard some chap from Hosidius went missing not too long ago. They reckon he went exploring the Stranglewood. Poor guy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who was he?

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: Some botanist I think. His name escapes me, but I think he lived just south of the Hosidius Estate Agent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... Thanks for all the information.

lines.speaker: Barus
lines.text: Don't mention it!

sections.title: Attempting to drink the potion before reading the note

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I probably shouldn't just go drinking random potions without knowing what they are.

sections.title: Boarding the rowboat before reading the note

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This Stranglewood place is supposed to be really dangerous. I should probably get myself some sort of protection before heading there.

sections.title: Boarding the rowboat before drinking the potion
Travelling to the Stranglewood for the first time

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just going to quietly go over here now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was a close one.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: And who on Gielinor are you?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: What are you waiting for? They're coming! Quick, head down below!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Well? Who are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm [player name], and you are?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I think you already know the answer to that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Do I?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Shaw. Kasonde Shaw. Don't go acting like you haven't been following me for some time now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, of course. Those old ruins.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Exactly. Perhaps you took me for a fool, but I was on to you from the start. Considering what we learnt, I knew it was only a matter of time before you showed up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean soul transference? Not actually something that interests me, to be honest.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Don't take me for a fool! If you had no interest in the Old Ones' secrets, why would you have followed me to those ruins? And why would you have come here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, I didn't even know what an Old One was. I still don't really. I was just exploring when I found your camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As for why I'm here, I'm looking for information about someone called Vardorvis.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Really? You want me to believe that all of this is a coincidence?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... yes.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: You're lying! You're lying and...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Hang on... do you hear that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hear what?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: The Strangled! Did you disturb any tendrils on your way in here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may have...?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: They'll be coming. We've got no chance up here. Head down below! Now!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: They're coming! Build some defences!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I hear them approaching from the north east!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: To the north east! They are coming!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Strangled coming in from the north east!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I hear them approaching from the north east!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: To the north west! They are coming!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Strangled coming in from the north west!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I hear them approaching from the south east!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: To the south east! They are coming!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Strangled coming in from the south east!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I hear them approaching from the south west!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: To the south west! They are coming!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Strangled coming in from the south west!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: They seem to be running out of steam! Let's head back up.

sections.title: If the player fails to protect Kasonde
Talking to Kasonde after holding back the horde

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: That's the lot of them. Let's head back up.

sections.title: Talking to Kasonde back on the surface
Talking to Kasonde again before retrieving the ingredients

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find those ingredients again?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: *sigh*

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I found the herb growing atop the cliff just north west of the temple. The berries were in an old camp on the coast to the south west.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see if I can find them.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: That was too close. You nearly got us both killed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand... What are those things? Are they zombies?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: You really want me to believe that you don't know anything about this place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already told you, I wasn't lying.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Well I don't have any reason to trust that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: With how dangerous this place is, do you really have a choice?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Alright, fine. But I have no desire to see you get us killed, so you'd better wisen up fast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm listening.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: It all starts with the Old Ones. They lived here long before humans came to these lands, and these ruins here were once their home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And these Old Ones discovered soul transference?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: They did, and they did so long before House Arceuus even existed. It was their final attempt to overcome their extinction, but they failed.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: What you see around you is the result of their failure. It's known as the Strangler.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Strangler?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: It's some sort of parasite. It was presumably meant to play a role in their transference rituals, but instead it destroyed them. It has remained here ever since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hence the Strangled?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Exactly. Plenty of unwary fools have fallen victim to the Strangler. If you don't take care, you will share their fate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do we deal with them?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: The trick is to maintain your distance. They're relatively passive unless you get too close, but if you disturb one, the rest will quickly come running.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: If you need to fight one, try to do it away from the rest!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: And stay off of the tendrils! You'll alert the lot of them to your location! It's like a network.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Keep my distance and stay off the tendrils. I can do that. So what's our next move?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: The fact that you're still alive tells me you broke into my home and stole some of the potion I made.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 'Broke in' is maybe a bit strong...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Call it what you want, it's not important now. What is important is that I believe I can make a more powerful potion using ingredients found here.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: If all goes well, this potion will provide increased immunity against the Strangler. If either of us are to find what we want here, it will be essential.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what ingredients do we need?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: As you've no doubt seen, the Strangler infects plant life as well. However, I've come across a small number of plants that seem to have developed immunity.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: First, there's a herb growing atop the cliff just north west of the temple. Second, there are some berries in an old camp on the coast to the south west.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I can gather those.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I need to gather some additional ingredients from within the temple. I will meet you back here once I'm done.

sections.title: Entering Kasonde's hideout after retrieving the serum ingredients

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... Kasonde must still be at the temple. Guess I should go and see if he needs a hand.

sections.title: Talking to Kasonde after killing the strangled in the temple
Talking to Kasonde after making the potion

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: We're running out of time! I'm running out of time! Drink that potion. It should strengthen your immunity against the Strangler. Now go!

sections.title: Talking to Kasonde after drinking the potion

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: We're running out of time! I'm running out of time! Get to the ritual site, now!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: We're running out of time! I'm running out of time! Take this potion and add those ingredients you gathered to it. It should strengthen your immunity against the Strangler. Now go!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kasonde! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Damn it! Damn it! This can't happen! It can't!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kasonde?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kasonde...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: It's fine! This doesn't change anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But the infection...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: The infection means nothing!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I was a dead man either way...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Koreps Syndrome. Common among botanists apparently. You'd have thought we'd have put some effort into finding a cure, but I guess no one ever thinks they'll be the unlucky one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kasonde, I...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Don't. Whatever you have to say, I don't want to hear it. Do you know how many times I've heard the word 'sorry'? Do you know how many times people have told me they understand?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Well they don't. I spent every day working away, telling myself that one day I'd finally be able to live my life, but it was all a lie. That was my life, and it was all so... meaningless!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I tried everything. I went to every healer, every mage, every priest.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I went to House Arceuus, and they turned me away! What gives them the right?!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: So no, this doesn't change anything. The Old Ones are my last hope. I either find their secrets, or...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Look, we're wasting time here! There's a ritual site to the west. Presumably it's where the Strangler was unleashed. It's my best hope now, and it may well be where you'll find what you're after as well.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Take this potion and add those ingredients you gathered to it. It should strengthen your immunity against the Strangler. Now go!

sections.title: Entering the temple after defeating Vardorvis

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Did you reach the ritual site? Did you find anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just a key.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: A key? Maybe it opens these doors... Quick, give it to me!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Right, I think I have something, but we need to go quickly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go? Go where?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Back to Kourend of course. You didn't think we'd be able to do this here, did you? I don't have any of the right equipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... the infection...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Yes, I'm all too aware! That's why we need to go now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what if you end up spreading it to Kourend?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: That's a risk we're going to have to take. We have no other option!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can't be serious! We're talking about thousands of lives here! How do you even know this will work? When the Old Ones tried, it killed them!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I haven't come this far to give up now! This is my last hope! If you won't help me, then step aside!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's too high a cost!

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I don't have time for this! I can already feel it inside me, changing me! Step aside... now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, Kasonde, but I can't do that.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Then you leave me no choice...

sections.title: Talking to Kasonde after defeating him

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: No... Not like this... Not when I'm so close...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Please... do something... Help me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry. I didn't want it to be this way, but I couldn't let you endanger all those people.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I just needed more time...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wish I could have helped you find it.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: At the ritual site... you didn't find what you we're looking for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: It wasn't a question. I knew you wouldn't. You didn't find it... because I already had it.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I had a feeling it was important, that it might have been what you were after, but if I'd given it to you, you wouldn't have helped me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You didn't know that.

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: I couldn't take the risk. You were my last hope...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kasonde...

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: The medallion. You'll find it in a chest back at my hideout. I hope... I hope it...

sections.title: Placing the medallion on the statue

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Hi.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: [player name]. I have an offer for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What offer?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: You have something that my mistress wants. Give it to me and you will be rewarded.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And if I refuse?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: I think you know what happens if you refuse.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was that a threat?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: I need an answer, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I know I can trust you?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: You don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This doesn't seem like a very good offer.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: It's the only offer you have. Now make your choice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I like a nice reward as much as the next person, but without any reason to trust you, I'm going to have to decline.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: I will see you soon.

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia Smith after placing one medallion

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Good to see you, kid. Nice work on that first statue. That treasure will be ours in no time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Once again, I never actually agreed to work with you.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Nonsense! Only a fool would say no to such a lucrative partnership, and you're clearly no fool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... If you say so.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Good to see you, kid. Have you had any luck with these statues yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a medallion that I think one of them should be holding.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well what are you waiting for? Go and give it to the statue! That twenty percent split won't earn itself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Once again, I never actually agreed to work with you.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Nonsense! Only a fool would say no to such a lucrative partnership, and you're clearly no fool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... If you say so.

sections.title: Talking to the Catalytic Guardian after exiting the Scar for the first time

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: Human... you survived the Scar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I did.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: How unexpected. The Great Guardian will need to be informed.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: In the meantime, do you wish to return to the Scar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: Very well. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: Speak to me again if you do.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: You are [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: The Great Guardian wants you to know that there has been a development in our fight against the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What development?

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: A magical signal has been detected emerging from the rift. It is not of the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then what is it?

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: Unknown.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I was actually hoping to enter the Scar myself. Perhaps I could investigate this signal while I'm in there.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: Nothing can survive the Scar. To enter would not be wise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how do you know nothing can survive? Has anyone actually confirmed that?

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: There are a few who have entered the Scar in the past. None have returned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then give me a chance to be the first.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: If you wish to enter, it is not our place to stop you. In the likely event of your demise, you will be mourned.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks... I think. Anything I should know before entering?

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: The rift is unpredictable. Normally, you would not be able to control its destination. However, the magical signal coming from within could be of benefit. It could act as a guide.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'd be able to send me right to the source?

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: Approximately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I suppose that's the best I can ask for.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: Are you ready to enter the Scar?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: Then may your death come quick, human. Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Catalytic Guardian
lines.text: Then speak to me once you are.

sections.title: Attempting to board the rowboat in the Scar

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well, look who it is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Persten! You're alive!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well of course I'm alive, silly. I'm Mia Persten! It takes more than the Abyss to kill me.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Speaking of which, it's a good job I found you. If you'd have got on that boat, you'd have become fish food!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fish food?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Absolutely! Come, I'll explain back at my camp.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Any luck with those ships yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: There should be one down each of those tunnels over there. Two are blocked right now, but I'll get that sorted while you work on the first one.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Welcome to my little home away from home. It's not much, but you take what you can get in a place like this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is it safe here?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Of course! You won't find anywhere safer in all the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well... that's probably not true, but let's pretend otherwise.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Still, the point is, the demons don't come down here thanks to our fishy friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I already had so many questions, but you keep giving me more. What's this fish thing you keep talking about?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: One of the many mysteries of this place. It's some sort of sea creature by the looks of it, but it's nothing like anything I've seen before.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It's very dangerous, and it's rather annoyingly preventing any navigation of that lovely lake of goo. Quite unfortunate as there's something out there that I really need to get to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well thankfully you shouldn't need to anymore.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm guessing you were responsible for that magic signal leaving this place? The Guardians of the Rift were able to use it to stabilise the rift. It's safe for you to leave now.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Leave? But I don't want to leave? I'm a leading expert on teleportation. I'd have had no problem getting out of here if I'd wanted to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Then why the signal?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Because I need to get past the big fishy, but I can't do it alone. I was hoping someone capable would follow the signal and join me. Looks like it worked perfectly!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why do you need to get past this fish... thing?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: There's a big old ship out there that I believe contains something I need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An old ship? Hmm...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does the name Perseriya mean anything to you?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Yes! It's her ship I'm trying to reach!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well she's actually why I'm here as well.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: And there I was thinking you'd come here just for me. Consider my feelings hurt. Still, seeing as we both want the same thing, how about we work together?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, how do we reach this ship?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: That fish creature needs dealing with. I've already tried myself, but its scales are stronger than anything I've ever encountered before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It must have some sort of weakness.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Indeed it does. There's a spot on its back where there's a gap in the scales.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's the catch?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Catch? Ha! Good one.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: The weak spot isn't easy to reach, especially with how quickly the creature can move around the lake. If we want to get close, we'll need to trap it. There's an island next to Perseriya's ship that should work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So what's your plan for trapping it?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I hope you like your fish smoked, because we're going to smoke this one out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It seems Perseriya was in charge of quite the fleet. Her ship isn't the only one in here. There's plenty of others about, still filled to the brim with all sorts of explosive materials.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, I see. You want to set those ships alight. Where can I find them?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: There's three that I know of, which should be enough for what we need. See those tunnels over there? There's one down each of them.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Two are blocked right now, but I'll get that sorted while you work on the first one. Once you set the ship alight, I'll make sure the smoke is directed right towards our fishy friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you survive in here?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It wasn't really that hard. Did you not think I would?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Guardians of the Rift didn't think you'd have any chance.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Guardians of the Rift? You mentioned them earlier. Who are they?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A group of ancient golems built to guard the Temple of the Eye. They've been keeping the rift under control since we opened it.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Ah, I'm guessing I won't be their favourite person then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it was sort of your fault.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It's not like it was intentional. Besides, we should look on the positive side. I've found all sorts of things in here to help with my research.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure they'd consider that to be worth the cost.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well even if we wanted to, we can't turn back time. We may as well just keep looking forward.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it you want with Perseriya's ship?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I reckon I'll find some information there to help with my research.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What information?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: If I knew that, I wouldn't need to go there, would I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But then how do you know there's information on her ship?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I found a tablet on another shipwreck down here. It suggested she'd been experimenting with teleportation magic. If that's true, her ship seems the best place to look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's with the demons in this place?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: To be honest, I'm not totally sure. It's clear the Abyss has demonic connections, hence, you know, Abyssal Demons. But Black Demons? Greater Demons?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It's obvious they don't belong here. The delightful denizens of the Abyss haven't taken kindly to their presence.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: From the rituals they keep performing, my guess is they're looking to harness the magic of the Abyss for themselves.

sections.title: Entering a passage where the player must swim

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not sure if this diving equipment is going to help in here. This... brain juice... is quite thick. I'll give it a go though.

sections.title: Attempting to burn the first ship prematurely

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably look around and see if I can find anything of interest before I go burning everything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably read this tablet I found before I go burning everything.

sections.title: Talking to Wizard Persten after completing the first passage

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: You're back! I'm guessing from all the smoke that came this way that all went as planned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It did.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Great! I've opened up the second tunnel for you. It's ready when you are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Before I go, I found something I wanted to ask you about.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found an old tablet near the ship. It mentioned two artefacts. One was referred to as Imperium and the other as Primus.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Really? They must be code names. I wonder what they refer to...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently, the one known as Primus was being defended by Saradominists. Perseriya and her fleet were trying to claim it, but things clearly didn't go as planned, hence them ending up here.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I don't follow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Isn't it obvious? It must be the Eye.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: You're starting to sound a bit accusational.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it's fair to say that you're normally a very upbeat person, but whenever the Eye is mentioned, you start to act... different. I think you know more than you're letting on.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hmm... Alright, you've got me.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Do you remember me telling you that my travels had taken me to other planes? One of them was a total wasteland. Ruins of a great civilisation as far as you could see.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Naturally, me being me, I was curious. I delved deep into the ruins to try and work out what happened.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Turns out, they were in possession of an artefact of immense power. It was capable of limitless creation, but also unimaginable destruction. It delivered them great prosperity, but also led to their ruin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This artefact... The Eye?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I think so. It's been known by many names, and most of them have been long forgotten. The Eye of Saradomin was one. The Fist of Guthix was another. But its true name is...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Stone of Jas.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: That's right... How did you know that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've seen it myself.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: You've encountered the Stone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but I knew about it even before I did. Do you remember that vision I had back in the Temple of the Eye? I saw it. I've somehow known its name ever since, but I just didn't realise until now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you remember that vision I had back in the Temple of the Eye? I saw it. I've somehow known its name ever since, but I just didn't realise until now.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: How fascinating... Makes sense though. After all, powerful magic leaves powerful traces.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So all that stuff about you searching for a teleportation nexus? That was a lie, wasn't it? You were really searching for information on the Stone.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Yeah... sorry about that. I had a feeling that if I suddenly popped up claiming to have information on a powerful artefact beyond anything ever encountered before, people might not believe me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess that does make sense. So is that what you're hoping to find on Perseriya's ship? Information on the Stone?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well I definitely wouldn't complain, but it's actually this other artefact I'm more interested in. The one referred to as Imperium.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I always assumed the Stone to be unique, but what if it's not? Could there be other artefacts of a similar power out there? I have to find out!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: And on that note, we should really get back to it. Those ships won't burn themselves!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about Imperium so far?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Before you came along, I'd already found quite a few mentions of it hidden in various shipwrecks. It seems that Perseriya possessed it for a time, but it was eventually transported somewhere to the east.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you think it's connected to the Stone of Jas?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I don't have anything solid, but multiple times it was compared to the Stone. Even if not directly connected, it seems the two were of a similar type of power.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Beyond that, I don't really have much. If we want to learn more, we need to reach Perseriya's ship.

sections.title: Attempting to burn the second or third ship prematurely

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's probably more answers lying around here. I'd better check before I burn it all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably read this tablet I found before I go burning everything.

sections.title: Talking to Wizard Persten after completing the second passage

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Welcome back. How did things go?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too bad. You might be interested in another tablet I found. It mentioned Imperium.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: How exciting! Well don't leave me hanging in anticipation. What did you find?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently a soldier interacted with the artefact at one point. It caused another soldier to enter a frenzy and kill multiple people.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Ah, that must have been a bit of a bother for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... both soldiers in question were later executed.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh dear... Still, this is highly useful information. We now know the artefact has the ability to control another, which is equal parts exciting and terrifying!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: The question now is, did the artefact do it on its own, or was it enacting the will of the soldier that touched it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess there's only one way to find out.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Indeed! Just one ship to go. I've managed to open up the final tunnel for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How powerful do you think Imperium is?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It's a curious one. We now know Imperium has the power to control another, but that doesn't really tell us much about the overall power of the artefact.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Mind control is a dangerous form of magic. The Order of Wizards forbid it for obvious reasons, but with enough skill, it is possible to learn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Mind controlling magic has a large number of drawbacks. All but the most powerful mages can only use it on one person at a time, and the spell is easily broken.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: But what if Imperium can overcome these limits? What if it can control hundreds, or even thousands, with no way to break that control!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's terrifying!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Indeed it is.

sections.title: Talking to Wizard Persten after completing the third passage

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: That big fish won't go sorting itself out. Just hop on the boat and row out to the island when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: First the Stone, and now this 'Imperium'. But that begs the question... how many more? So silly of me! I spent so much time focusing on the Stone, I never even considered the possibility...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... hello?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh, you're back! Sorry, I was in my own little world there, chittering away. Anyway, looks like our plan worked. I was able to use all that smoke to force our big fish towards that island!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So now we just need to properly deal with it.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: That's right! Now don't worry, I have a very in-depth plan. Basically, one of us needs to be ready to direct the smoke in case it tries to escape while the other goes and gives it a good old poke!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I'll be doing the poking?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Do you have the magical abilities to maintain a huge whirlwind of smoke that's strong enough to prevent a large aquatic lifeform from passing through?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Then yes, you'll be doing the poking!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I guess we should get to it.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Absolutely! Oh, don't forget there's a weak spot on its back that you'll want to try and target. I can't see it letting you get too close, so I reckon you'll want to try and stun it first.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: From my experiences in this place, some sort of shadowy spell should do the trick! I'm sure a capable individual like you has access to something suitable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... How do you know about...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Because I'm Mia Persten, silly! You don't get as far as me without knowing a thing or two about forbidden magic. Now, just hop on the boat and row out to the island when you're ready. Oh, and try not to die!

sections.title: Crossing the stones after defeating the Leviathan

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Alright, let's see what we've got...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Jackpot! Now let's see...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Huh? That can't be right...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It can't be possible... can it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Persten? What does it say?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It's written by one of the crew on Perseriya's ship. Apparently they snuck into her private cabin...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: And found Imperium inside!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's not right about that? Makes sense she'd have kept it close.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: This was after Imperium had already been delivered to the east!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it was never delivered? But that can't be right... We've found multiple tablets saying it was.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Exactly! That either means she was able to sneak it back onto the ship again, or there was a second artefact!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could that be possible?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I don't know... I don't think so... But then again, until recently I didn't even know about this artefact at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I'm not too sure. I need to gather all my research and see what I can come up with. I guess that means that I've outlived my little home here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're going back to the Wizards' Tower?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well... maybe not just yet. I'll have to face the music there at some point, but probably best I wait until after I've got my head around all this Imperium business.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: If you don't mind, I'd appreciate it if you'd not mention what we found here to Sedridor and the others until I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough, but you do need to tell them soon.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh don't worry, I will. Now, I'd best be getting off. I'm sure I'll see you again before long. I hope you find what you're looking for here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, Persten. Good luck.

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia Smith after placing two medallions

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Good to see you, kid. Nice work on those two statues. That treasure will be ours in no time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Once again, I never actually agreed to work with you.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Nonsense! Only a fool would say no to such a lucrative partnership, and you're clearly no fool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... If you say so.

sections.title: Duke Sucellus of the Sixth Legion

lines.speaker: Captain Malleta
lines.text: What do you want, adventurer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to enter the prison.

lines.speaker: Captain Malleta
lines.text: No. General Khazard has ordered us to guard this door. I'm not about to just let you walk through it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you're guarding the door because of that assassin, right? Why does that mean I can't go in?

lines.speaker: Captain Malleta
lines.text: I don't have time to answer your questions. I've told you that you can't go in, so go away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you not bored of being on guard here? It can't be much fun in this dingy cold dungeon. Food must be a bit meh as well.

lines.speaker: Captain Malleta
lines.text: What is your point?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If that assassin were to be dealt with, Khazard might reconsider your posting. Rather than sitting here waiting around, why not let me go in and find him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Besides, let's be honest, if I die doing so, Khazard would probably be thrilled.

lines.speaker: Captain Malleta
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Captain Malleta
lines.text: Fine. If it stops you bothering me, feel free to go and get yourself killed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Lovely! Thank you for your time.

sections.title: Inspecting the wall in the prison cell

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Any luck in there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Keep at it. There must be something we can use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a letter on a skeleton.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Well read it and see what it says!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a letter on a skeleton.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: What does it say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Someone was trying to help some prisoners escape. Might be we can use whatever they left behind to our advantage.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Brilliant! Keep looking and see what else you can find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am not sticking my hand in there without good reason!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Eugh! I can't believe I'm doing this.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Hey! Can you hear me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Hello?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Ah, there you are!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you? Are you one of Khazard's soldiers?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: That's right! Made the mistake of sticking my nose where I shouldn't have. Now I've got myself a lovely little cell and no way to get out of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah... Seems I've got myself into a similar situation.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Well, I tell you what, now that you're here, maybe we have a chance to escape!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: No idea, but I'm sure we can come up with something. Anything in your cell that you reckon could be of use?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure. Let me take a look.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Great!

sections.title: Searching the bed with a knife
Talking to the mysterious voice after getting a knife

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that chisel you gave me.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: What? How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh wait, it's on the floor here. Silly me.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Any luck in there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to find a knife.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Oh, pass it through! I reckon I can make use of it.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Right, give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Okay, I have something! See if you can find a use for this.

sections.title: Searching the bed with a chisel
Talking to the mysterious voice after getting a lockpick

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: How's it going in there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm getting close I reckon.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Good stuff!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to find a lockpick.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Oh, I'm pretty good with those! Pass it through and I'll see if I can get my cell unlocked. Once I'm out, I can free you as well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You! I should have known!

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Yes, you probably should have.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Give me one good reason why I shouldn't kill you right now!

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I can give you two. First, because you'll fail. You have no weapons or armour, and even if you did, it wouldn't go well. Second, because without me you'd still be in that cell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Without me you'd be in a cell as well!

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Correct. Now, while I appreciate you came here to kill me, we are both in each other's debt, so let's set aside the threats and start focusing on more important matters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well actually I didn't come here for you. I'm looking for Duke Sucellus.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently he used to be in charge of this place. That letter I found mentioned the asylum, which seems a good place to start my search.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I suspect your search will be quite a short one as things currently stand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: It seems you are yet to consider an obvious question. Are you not wondering how I ended up in that cell?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... yes, I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: It was that Mahjarrat. Seems he doesn't think much of you either, as he was also responsible for your own imprisonment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jhallan? But he was your prisoner!

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: He was, but now he's not. There was an unfortunate mishap.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A mishap? Nice work.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where is he now?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I don't know, but he'll be nearby, and we are currently poorly equipped to deal with him. I'm missing my sword and you don't have any weapons either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you see where he put my things?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: They're in a chest in the cell opposite this one, but the cell is locked. My sword is in there as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do I feel like you have a proposal?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Because I do, and you'd be wise to listen to it. I came here in search of something, and the asylum is one of the last places I need to look.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: With Jhallan around, neither of us stand a good chance of getting what we want. However, if we work together, we can deal with him and get to the asylum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I have no reason to trust you.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: No, but you don't have any other options. Now, I suggest you focus on finding the key to that cell so we can recover our equipment. While you do that, I'll work out how to reach the asylum.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Oh, and you should take this. You might need it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, slow down there...

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: We'll reconvene once you have the cell open.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh*

sections.title: Seaching the books in the administration library

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: I have you now!

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: There you are!

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: Did you think I wouldn't find you?

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: Going somewhere?

lines.speaker: Jhallan
lines.text: You won't escape me!

sections.title: Talking to the assassin after getting the diamond key

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Do you have the key?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Good. Let's get this cell open.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. First, you have to give me some answers.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Rather than resorting to needless anger, had you considered just asking first?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... well, why are you here?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I told you, I'm looking for something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: A magical artefact called the Frostenhorn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Frostenhorn?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: You've heard of it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard the name, but I don't know anything about it. What is it?

lines.speaker: Assassin
