lines.text: I don't know and I don't need to know. I've been paid to recover it from this place and that's all that matters to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Paid by who?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: That's confidential.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did this person also pay you to steal that relic from Entrana?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: They did.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what about all that business with the Hazeel Cult and the Carnilleans?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I needed to recover that crest half from the Carnillean Mansion. Framing the Hazeel Cult was the best cover.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why have Hazeel resurrected as part of that?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Because it made it believable. Now, are we done?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why try to have Hazeel resurrected as part of that?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Because it would have made it believable. Unfortunately, I failed to account for you having already done so. Now, are we done?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I guess so.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: You seem surprised by that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I wasn't expecting you to be so open about it all.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: You weren't going to open the gate until I provided satisfactory answers, so I provided them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not try to kill me instead? It's not like that's ever been a problem for you before.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I kill when it is the best way to further my goal. That would not have been the case here, so I chose the cleaner and more efficient option.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Besides, beyond placating your curiosity, the answers I gave are rather meaningless. They do not jeopardise me or my employer in any way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh... Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Now, once again, let's get this cell open.

sections.title: Talking to the assassin again before getting your items back

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Time is wasting. You should get this cell open.

sections.title: Talking to the assassin after getting your items back

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Right, you'll be pleased to know that I've obtained a key that can open the way to the asylum. However, Jhallan must be dealt with before we can use it safely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I don't know much about the Mahjarrat, but it's clear they're not easy to kill. Jhallan is thankfully fairly weak for their kind, but that still makes him significantly stronger than us.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Our best option will be to trap him. I suggest we do so by repeating what we did before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What we did before?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Yes. You distract him and I take him unaware.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you talking about what happened back in that cave? There was no 'we' about that.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: You provided an opportunity and I took it. Your awareness of said opportunity was irrelevant.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... So how do I distract him?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: The old refugee camp seems like a good place to spring our trap. I left a pack there with some firecrackers in. The noise should quickly attract his attention.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Once he's there, don't try to be a hero. Just keep him busy until I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that it?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Yes. Now, let's be on our way.

sections.title: Searching the pack in the refugee camp
Talking to the assassin after Jhallan is frozen

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Right, that's him dealt with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will that ice hold?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: It should, but it would be unwise to wait and find out. I must resume my search for the Frostenhorn and you have your Duke business to deal with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To the asylum then?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Yes. You can get there by heading north. I've already unlocked the gate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is becoming quite tiresome.

sections.title: Talking to the assassin after unlocking the asylum office

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Curious...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I've checked every corner of this prison now. This room was the last place to look. There is only one possibility: The Frostenhorn isn't here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if it's not here, then where is it?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: A good question, but one that is not my concern. My employer believed it to be here, but was clearly mistaken. That's their problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't mind that you failed?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I did all that was required of me. If they are able to discover the true location of the horn, they are welcome to hire me once more. Until then, my work is done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you think I'm going to let you go? You have a whole lot of murders you need to answer for.

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: I will not be giving you a choice in the matter. Perhaps justice will be served someday, but not today. Goodbye.

sections.title: Talking to Asgarnia Smith after placing three medallions

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: We're getting close now, kid. Just one more statue to go! I can smell that treasure already!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Once again, I never actually agreed to work with you.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Nonsense! You'd be a fool to turn away now that we're so close! I'm about to make history - and you'll have helped!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... If you say so.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen any archeologists around here?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Human archeologists? Yes, some showed up recently. I told them they'd be fine to look around as long as they were careful. They're probably over by the bank chest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Helena
lines.text: Hello there. Did you see that dwarf by the big forge? I think he wanted to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Helena
lines.text: Hello. Sorry, I'd love to stop and chat, but I have a new find that I need to work on. If you need anything, I'm sure Prescott can help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Helena
lines.text: Not a problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. Where did you say that hole was?

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: You'll find it just on the other side of the mine. You'll need a pretty long rope to get down there though. Here, you can use mine.

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: You'll find it just on the other side of the mine. Be careful down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Talking to Prescott after entering the undercity

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: Hello! Glad to see you're okay. I was worried you might have gone and got stuck down that hole.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No need to worry. I'm mostly quite capable when it comes to these kinds of things!

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: Good to hear!

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: Hello there. Did you see that dwarf by the big forge? I think he wanted to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: Hello! Look at this place. Isn't it amazing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sure is! Not every day you find yourself in a lost city.

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: So what can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does the name Lassar mean anything to you? It was an old fortress that once existed here, long before Camdozaal. I'm wondering if there might still be some traces left down here.

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: A ruin within a ruin? How exciting! We have found the odd thing that doesn't seem to be dwarven in origin, but it's still early days for us here, so we haven't been able to learn much yet.

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: One that was particularly interesting was this strange icon I found in the mine.

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: Could be a lot more to find over there, but I must admit, I haven't been back since... well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Since what?

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: It's probably nothing, but there's this big sinkhole on the other side of the mine. I'm probably just being silly, but I'm sure I heard a voice coming from inside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting... And if I wanted to go down this hole?

lines.speaker: Prescott
lines.text: Go down the hole?! You're far braver than I am! There's plenty of rocks around there. I'm sure you could tie a rope to one of them. You'd need a pretty long rope though. Here, you can use mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Talking to Ketla after coming to

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Huh? What happened? Where am I? And who are you?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: I am Ketla. I found you wandering the streets. It nearly had you. You were nearly lost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It? What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: An unknowable stranger with a thousand faces. An endless ocean where the lost swim. A fissure in fate that exists beyond time.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It twists its way into your very soul, devouring all that you are, until by the time it's done, only one question remains...

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Do you think you can save them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Oh... I'm sorry. Sometimes it becomes difficult to resist the call. It gets harder with every day that goes by.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Resist what? It?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes. It wants my soul, and I fear it will soon have it. My strength is leaving me and I cannot hold it back. This city will be my doom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But then why not leave? I'm guessing you're an Imcando dwarf, right? A survivor from Camdozaal? Others of your kind still live. You could leave this place and join them.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Others still live? I feared I was the last...

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Well then that is all the more reason to stay. For the good of my people, for all people, it has to be stopped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could help? I'm a pretty good fighter. I'm sure I can deal with whatever this thing is.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Your weapons are worthless. It is not an evil to be slain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then how can we stop it?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: The totem. It links our world to the shadows... to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The shadows? As in the Shadow Realm?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: You know of the shadows?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: More than know. I've been there! I have a ring that lets me do so.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: A ring? I must see it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. I don't have it with me, but I can go and fetch it.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes. Please bring it to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course. I have it right here.

sections.title: Talking to Ketla with the ring of visibility

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: You will need this.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Do you have the ring?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Where did you get this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A wandering merchant gave it to me. I never asked how he got it.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: There is a great power to this ring, but the shadows here reject it. It must know the ring is a threat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So the ring is useless?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: On its own, yes. The ring can see into the shadows, but it is not of the shadows. However, I have something that is.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: A shard of the totem. Too dangerous to use on its own, but with the power of this ring, perhaps I can make it safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like it's worth a go.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: There. The fragment should be safe to use now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what can we do with it?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: You said the ring let you enter the shadows, but that is not the case. It simply allowed you a small glimpse into their darkness.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: But it rules the shadows. To overcome it, you must enter the shadows as well. The fragment will allow you to do so now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can use this fragment to enter the Shadow Realm? But surely there's going to be a lot of danger entering the domain of this... whatever it is.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes. Once you enter the shadows, you will be at its mercy. Unless you long for the eternal sea, you would be wise not to stay for long.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's... concerning.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It is the only way. You must enter the shadows and destroy the totem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But where is the totem?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Its power is strongest in the sunken cathedral. That is likely where the totem will be found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I'm guessing I won't just be able to walk on in there?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: No. It has blocked the way. You will need to clear the path with cleansing flames.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cleansing flames? Like a tinderbox?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: The fire of our world will do nothing in the shadows, but the old inhabitants of this city, they tried to fight off the inevitable darkness. They created things that could help you.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: If you find their old schematics, bring them to me. I can use them to create new tools for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: The fragment won't just work anywhere. To enter the shadows, you must use it where they are strongest. Seek out the places where that is the case. You will know them when you see them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what about leaving again?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: That is thankfully easier. The fragment will let you leave the shadows whenever you desire. Be sure to use it before darkness claims you.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you end up down here?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: You know of Camdozaal. That means you have seen what became of our home.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: When the barbarians came, many tried to fight. I should have fought with them, but I turned away. I had a duty to my people, but I abandoned them.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: The barbarians had blocked all exits, so the only way to flee was down. I hid down here while my people died, but now I pay the price for my choice.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: There are worse things than death, and this city is home to them all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You shouldn't feel guilty for fleeing. Your people had no hope against the barbarians.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: You misunderstand. Fleeing was not the cause of my guilt. My punishment is for what happened next...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It is best you do not know. Please, let us speak of something else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about this city?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Lassar. That is what they called this place. It was once home to some of the greatest minds in the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I knew about the fortress of Lassar, but I had no idea a city was here as well.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It seems they kept it hidden on purpose. This city was a place of discovery, where magic and science were practiced in equal measure. They did not want their secrets falling into the wrong hands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess that's how this place is so well preserved. No one even knew it was here.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes. They remained hidden down here long after their fortress fell. They were still here even after the construction of Camdozaal.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Their isolation kept them safe for a time, but when darkness fell, there was no one to hear their screams.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me any more about this entity?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It is beyond our understanding. It exists in the shadows, and there it should remain, but the blackstone is like a blade. It cuts through into our world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Blackstone?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It comes from the shadows as well. It holds a great power, and many have tried to use it, not knowing the cost of doing so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that what happened here?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes. They were studying the totem, but in doing so, they followed in our footsteps, dooming their city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Followed in your footsteps? But it wasn't blackstone that destroyed Camdozaal, was it?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Camdozaal was not the first home of my people. From a young age, we are all taught of the Fall of Imcandoria, of the disaster that claimed our city.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: But down here, I have seen the truth. It was no volcano that destroyed our home, it was the totem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The same totem that's now here?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Did it follow us, or did we follow it? Are we fated to be its victims forevermore? Or will the cycle end here? It will soon be time to find out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: [player name]...

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is an avalanche of horrors which has discovered your name.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a bard who sings of your final moments.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a blood-soaked record of your darkest moments.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a choir of souls who you will one day meet.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a city of blackstone where the screams are ever silent.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is an empire of darkness where hope flickers and dies.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a judge who has already passed your sentence.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a library of sorrow where your final fate is written.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a monument built in honour of the suffering you desire.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is an ocean of souls where you are one day fated to swim.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is an open wound that bleeds only darkness.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a priest who prays for the oceans of the dead.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a reaper who has a thousand gibbering faces.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a song so loud that you only hear silence.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a stranger who wears a black mask under their skin.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: There is a void that echoes with the cries of those you failed to save.

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: Your fate is sealed, [player name], and only one question remains...

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: You only have one future, [player name], so ask yourself this...

lines.speaker: Strange Voice
lines.text: Do you really think you can save them?

sections.title: Talking to Ketla with a schematic

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a schematic here.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Let me see.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes, I think I can work with this. Give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: There. You can collect your new item from the workbench. Let me know if you want any information on how it works.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Ketla about a device

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about one of these items?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Which one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about the Shadow Torch?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It is a light that will aid you in the shadows, where all other lights fail. It is the only thing that can destroy what lurks there. Use it well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about the Shadow Blocker?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Almost nothing can stop you from being consumed by the shadows, but set the Shadow Blocker down, and it will provide a small area of safety. It's the only safety you'll get.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about the Revitalising Idol?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Unless you wish for the void, you must not spend too long in the shadows. Once set down, you can use the Revitalising Idol to give you more time there. But remember, time still always runs out in the end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me more about the Anima Portal?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Those that lurk in the shadows do not take kindly to we that still live. They will hunt you. Use the Anima Portal to give them something else to hunt. It provides a glimmer of life in their sea of emptiness.

sections.title: Viewing the remnant in the pub

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 1
lines.text: Are you ready, my child?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: I... I think so.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: He has doubts.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: No! It's just...

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 1
lines.text: Do not be afraid, my child, for you are not alone. Your voice will be one of thousands, singing an eternal silence.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: What do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 1
lines.text: Seek out our divine order in the cistern below. They will guide you across the waves.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 1
lines.text: Go now, and keep our icon close, for it marks you as one of the chosen. Without it, you are forsaken.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 2
lines.text: Okay... I'll go right away!

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 1
lines.text: And so another voice joins the Silent Choir.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 3
lines.text: He is afraid. He is not ready.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice 1
lines.text: It matters not. The day of ascension approaches. His fear will be washed away by the endless sea...

sections.title: Talking to Ketla with a schematic

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a schematic here.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Let me see.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Yes, I think I can work with this. Give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: This torch schematic. It's a fake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fake? But how?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It is marked by the black hand. This is the doing of the Silent Choir.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Silent Choir?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: They were the first to fall. It corrupted them and made them its own. They undid the work of those that tried to resist. Harbingers for what was to come.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So they stole the real schematic? Where would they have taken it?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: You ask many questions. I have one for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Is now the best time?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: You did not come to help, but you do so anyway. Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for information on someone known as the Whisperer. I have reason to beleve she was here before the city fell.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: There are many whispers in this city, and it hears them all. Will you join them? Will you join the Silent Choir?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ketla? Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It sees you, [player name]. Did you really think you could hide? Did you really think you could save them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ketla!

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Huh? What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay? You were in some sort of trance.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: It knows. It knows what we are trying to do. We must hurry. Soon it will be impossible to resist.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: The Silent Choir. They had a church. They built it somewhere in the city. Somewhere hidden.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I think it might be in the cistern beneath us.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: How do you know this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something I found earlier. However, it also seemed to suggest that some sort of icon is needed to enter the church.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Then you must find one of these icons. Be quick. Time is not on our side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then we have to find it. Can you think of anywhere that would be a good place to start?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: The inn. They met there. Hiding in plain sight. Perhaps they left something behind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's worth a shot. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Be quick. Time is not on our side.

sections.title: Talking to Ketla with the icon

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: You have the icon? Good. You should be able to reach the cistern using the drain in the square outside. Hurry.

sections.title: Inspecting the totem in the cistern

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: I will be departing this place soon. Be careful in the cathedral, [player name].

sections.title: Talking to Ramarno before learning the truth about Ketla

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found another dwarf!

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: A dwarf? An Imcando Dwarf?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems so. Her name is Ketla. I found her in a hidden Zarosian ruin beneath us.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ketla...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know her?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I did, long ago. I thought she was gone. Tell me... was she alone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She was.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I see... How is she?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's... okay. Things haven't been great for her, but I think the worst is behind her.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: That's good. I hope I get to meet her soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to the other Imcando Dwarves?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah... It's a tough tale to tell. Even though it's been many years since the last of my kin died out, the memory is still fresh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't have to tell me if the memory hurts too much.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: No, I will. The legacy of my kin must live on.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: As you can see around you, Camdozaal was once a great city. Alive with the hum of activity. We dug deep into the mountain and reaped the rewards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Our methods would often differ from other dwarves. Whereas they just used traditional smithing techniques, we would combine them with magic as well.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Alas, that was ultimately our undoing. We learned of a horde of barbarians pillaging across the land, hunting down and killing anyone who dared use magic.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: We underestimated them. We believed our weapons would be enough to keep us safe. We were wrong.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The barbarians brought ruin upon Camdozaal. Most of my kind died as the city collapsed around them. Only a few of us survived until the end. Now, only I remain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The world has changed a lot, Ramarno. Magic is practiced freely. Those barbarians failed to wipe it from Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I'm glad they didn't win, I suppose. But it doesn't change much for me. The Imcando are all but extinct. I am one of the last to remain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But not the last. Don't forget Ketla.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But not the last. Don't forget Thurgo and Ketla.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Yes... You're right, [player name]. I should not be so down. There is still a chance my kind can live on.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is an open wound that bleeds only darkness.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a bard who sings of your final moments.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is an avalanche of horrors which has discovered your name.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a stranger who wears a black mask under their skin.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a city of blackstone where the screams are ever silent.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a monument built in honour of the suffering you desire.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a choir of souls who you will one day meet.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a song so loud that you only hear silence.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a library of sorrow where your final fate is written.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a reaper who has a thousand gibbering faces.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is an atrocity written upon blackstone.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a king who presides over a court of corpses.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is an ocean of souls where you are one day fated to swim.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a judge who has already passed your sentence.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this? What's happening?

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: There is a truth written upon the blackstone, and you cannot escape it.

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: Come, [player name]. The Silent Choir awaits you. It is time for your eternal song.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This isn't real! I know this isn't real!

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: You cannot escape your fate, [player name]. The endless sea calls for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop it!

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: Do you really think you can hide, [player name]?

lines.speaker: [player name]
lines.text: Do you really think you can save them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Help me!

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The totem! I... What happened?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: You were gone too long. I came to find you, but it already had you. You had fallen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then how did you save me?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: The totem. I destroyed it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You did? So it's over?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: No... I thought destroying the totem would be enough, but it's already too late. It has claimed this place now. We can never undo that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But surely destroying the totem did something?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Its influence has been weakened. Without the totem, it cannot spread beyond this city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then you still achieved what you set out to do, right? The surviving Imcando are safe now. You can leave.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: I thought destroying the totem would be the end. I thought it would bring peace...

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: But there is no peace. I still hear their screams. I will never be free of what I did to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Good. Pray that you never have to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: No more of this. I found the missing schematic in the church. Your new tool is ready. Perhaps you can use it to find what it is you seek here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The cathedral is the only place I haven't searched.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: There is a darkness there. I thought it was the totem, but clearly not. Something else lurks in those depths...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I'd best be careful.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: I will not be here when you return. My journey in this place is done. It is time to start a new one. I will leave my things on the workbench for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, Ketla, for everything. Whatever it is you're looking for... I hope you eventually find it.

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Farewell, [player name]. Perhaps we will meet again.

sections.title: Entering the Ancient Vault with the last medallion

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Pathetic.

sections.title: Inspecting the altar in the temple

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: You escaped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seems I have a knack for that sort of thing. Maybe you underestimated me.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Unlikely. You have displayed moderate skill at best.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I assume you have the medallion?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: I do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I'm guessing you're not about to just give it to me?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Correct. You are left with two options.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: If you wish, I will allow you to accept your defeat and walk away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Or?

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: I have gone too long without a worthy challenger. You say I underestimated you? Prove it. Show me a true fight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm not backing down now.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Figure
lines.text: Very well...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Blimey, kid. You don't half take your time, do you? That treasure of ours won't uncover itself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ours? Like I said before, I never actually agreed to work with you.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: What? You're not thinking of double-crossing me, are you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't double-cross you if I was never working with you to begin with! Besides, we have more important things to worry about than treasure.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Oh, I see what's happening! You're trying to trick me so you can take the treasure for yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Listen to me! We're not the only ones trying to get into this place! There was this woman who kept trying to steal the medallions. Someone sent her!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Well of course there will be others who want the treasure! All the more reason for you to stop messing around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not going any further until we work out what's going on.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: I really thought you had what it takes, kid, but you're just another fool. If you won't help me find the treasure, I'll just have to do it myself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What in the world are you playing at?

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Look at it... It's... beautiful!

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Do you feel that? There's a power radiating off it unlike anything I've felt before...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This power... I've felt it before...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: So you know what it is?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not exactly. The last time I felt this power, it was something very different creating it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That said, I reckon I do have some idea as to what this is.

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Frostenhorn. I've no idea what it does, but it's a powerful Zarosian artefact that it seems quite a few people are searching for.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: The Frostenhorn has the ability to amplify power to potentially unimaginable levels.

lines.speaker: Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Its rediscovery would be revolutionary, but there's just one problem...

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: That's not the Frostenhorn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dr Banikan? You followed me here?

lines.speaker: Dr Banikan
lines.text: I did. You must forgive me, friend, for I'm afraid I've deceived you in another way as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Azzanadra?!

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: My apologies, but my subterfuge was necessary. There are far too many who would have tried to stop me recovering the Frostenhorn.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: After our earlier encounter, I came to believe it might be here, but it seems I was mistaken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if this isn't the Frostenhorn, then what is it?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: There are many powerful artefacts in this world, but few are truly unknowable. This is one of those few. A relic that predates even the gods themselves.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: It has had many names over many millennia, but to us, it was simply known as the Elder Horn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does it do?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: It grants absolute control. With this, one person has the power to bring entire armies to their knees, all without a single drop of blood being shed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... I think I've heard of this artefact. You say it's been known by many names? Was one of them Imperium?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: You are well informed. Perhaps then you also know that the Horn was supposedly sent far to the east, which begs the question as to how it stands before us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Because there were two.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something I discovered while tracking down Perseriya. It didn't make sense then, but it does now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one she took east was a fake. She secretly kept the real one. The intention must always have been to hide it here.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: A clever deception, although it does raise another question. If the Frostenhorn was never here, then where is it?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Azzanadra. It's been a while.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Hazeel. I thought I sensed someone lurking in the shadows. How long have you been here?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Long enough. And in answer to your question, the Frostenhorn was destroyed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Destroyed? How?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: I was hoping you might be able to enlighten us, considering you were there when it happened.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Yes. It was during your little excursion to Ghorrock Prison.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Enough talk, Hazeel. We have business to attend to. The Elder Horn will be claimed in Zamorak's name.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Enakhra. You knew the Elder Horn was here?

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Of course not! Do you really think we'd have left it here for so long if that were the case? We are not fools.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Speaking of fools, I know he's here as well! Reveal yourself!

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: Gladly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Were all of you just waiting for me to open this place?

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: We had no choice. This vault was sealed with magics that prevented beings like ourselves from opening it.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: It didn't have to be that way, Hazeel. We had proxies available. Had you supported my plan, we'd have the Horn by now.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Unlikely. Your minion failed to recover even one of the medallions.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: What a surprise. Enakhra coming up with a flawed plan as usual.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Would you like me to turn you into a boneguard again, you camel-loving coward?

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: I'd like to see you try. Come, Azzanadra. We can make short work of these traitors and claim the Horn for Zaros.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: You Zarosians have no hope of withstanding the power of two Zamorakian Mahjarrat. The Horn is ours.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Two? We are not the fools you take us for, Enakhra. I can sense five Mahjarrat here, but I only see four.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: Who are you hiding? Khazard? He always loved lurking in the shadows.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Khazard is attending to business in the North. Surely it is you who is hiding someone.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: We do not hide like you do. We came alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if you all came alone...

lines.speaker: Asgarnia Smith
lines.text: Oh... hello.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Sliske. I should have known.

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Azzanadra.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: The Serpent himself. Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Why am I here?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Well I'm here for the Horn.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: You think you can just take it?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Of course. My friend here and I worked hard for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not your friend! I don't even know you!

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: No? I think you do. I think you know me better than anyone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: You and I have been friends for some time now. Don't you remember?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Don't you remember how we survived the horrors of the Scar?

lines.speaker: Assassin
lines.text: Don't you remember how we escaped Ghorrock Prison?

lines.speaker: Ketla
lines.text: Don't you remember how we held back the shadows of Lassar?

lines.speaker: Kasonde
lines.text: Don't you remember how we overcame the infected Stranglewood?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... they were never real? It was all a lie? It was always just you?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: A lie? No. They were all so real, until their lives were cut short. Their stories would have ended, but I stepped in.

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: I became them and they became me. In many ways, they're the real me, far more so than the form before you now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You used them to manipulate me! Why?

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: He needed you to open the vault.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he didn't need me to get those medallions! He already had one of them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Were you just toying with me?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Toying? No. It was a test.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What test?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: To see if you were worthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of what?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Of me.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: You should have contacted me, Sliske. You should have told me about the Horn.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: He clearly wanted it for himself.

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: No, not myself. I want the Horn for all of us.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: What nonsense are you on about?

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Do you not see our fate? Over and over, we have our ritual. Over and over, we doom one of our kind to the endless void.

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: It's all so... predictable. It's all so... tiresome. And in the end, it will be the death of us all.

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: But the Horn will be our salvation. I will use it to break the cycle that has shackled us for so long. We will finally be free.

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: The ritual approaches once more, but things will be different this time. This will be the final Ritual of the Mahjarrat.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: He's finally lost it. We can take him. He's alone.

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: I'm never alone.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: You will not be taking that Horn, Enakhra. Not while I stand.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Then I'll just have to kill you first!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop! You're not going anywhere with that horn!

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Don't you see, [player name]? I can't stop now. Do you not see our future? Only with the Horn can we make it happen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whatever future you're planning, it ends here!

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: End? No. This is just the beginning. In time, you will see the truth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I doubt that!

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: Perhaps some more familiar faces will change your mind.

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: You fought well, [player name]. You will be a worthy vessel.

lines.speaker: Sliske
lines.text: I'll see you in the North.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: We have failed.

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: Hmph!

lines.speaker: Enakhra
lines.text: I'll see you at the ritual, Akthanakos. Perhaps this one will be your last.

lines.speaker: Akthanakos
lines.text: Our allies must be informed of this. Come, Azzanadra.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: It seems your pet has broken free of his leash, Azzanadra.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: You know as much as I that Sliske serves no one but himself. His actions are his own.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Yet you have shielded him for centuries. A choice that all of us may now suffer from.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: You can blame me as much as you wish, Hazeel. It is irrelevant. Sliske now holds the Elder Horn and we must be ready to oppose him.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: We? You made your choice a long time ago, Azzanadra. There is no 'we' anymore.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: And yet you yourself admit that Sliske poses a threat to us all. The prospect of working with Zamorakians gives me no pleasure, but it may be the only option.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Perhaps. Perhaps not. Time will soon tell.

lines.speaker: Hazeel
lines.text: Now, we're done here. I'll see you soon, Azzanadra.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Well, adventurer, it seems I owe you an apology.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: I did not show you the trust that you deserved. You freed me from my prison, a feat even my closest followers failed to achieve, and yet I doubted you.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: I did not know your allegiance, and so I was cautious, but it is clear that focusing on allegiances has blinded us. Sliske took advantage of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well thank you, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Now, I must ask about the destruction of the Frostenhorn. Sliske's doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It must have been.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Then I fear the other artefacts will have met a similar fate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Other artefacts?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: The Frostenhorn was one of three powerful items I was seeking. Together, they may have been my best chance to determine the fate of my lord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Sliske didn't want that?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Apparently not, though who could say why. I would never describe Sliske as a true believer, but until now, he always supported the Empty Lord.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Indeed, he himself was entrusted with another of the three artefacts. An icon that was kept under close guard by those wights of his.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah... Well unless they were guarding multiple icons, I'm afraid Sliske destroyed that one as well. As for your third artefact, it didn't happen to be on Entrana, did it?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: I had yet to track it down, but that does seem likely. It was stolen by Saradominists long ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I think it's safe to say it's been destroyed too. Sliske stole it from Entrana while disguised as that assassin.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: So my worst fears are confirmed. This may well be the darkest day for us since the fall of the Empty Lord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you do now?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Since you freed me, I have been driven by a singular purpose. I exist for Zaros. I exist for the Empire. Sliske thinks he has destroyed that purpose, but I will not give up.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: However, that is for another day. There are more immediate matters to concern myself with now. The Ritual of the Mahjarrat is fast approaching, and whatever Sliske is planning will happen there.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: I must ensure I am prepared, and while it may not have been your intention, you are part of this now. You must also ensure you are ready. To help with this, I have something to offer you.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: I understand you are in possession of a ring that lets you see into the Shadow Realm? Considering Sliske's mastery over that place, that is a power that will likely be of great use to you.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: Allow me to upgrade it with some offensive and defensive enchantments. They may well give you an important edge if you find yourself fighting in the shadows.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you. Do you need me to fetch the ring for you?

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: No need. I can summon it with ease.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Azzanadra
lines.text: And on that note, I must leave you, my friend. May you walk in the shadow of the Empty Lord.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have news on Persten.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but she's gone.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I see. It was always unlikely that she'd survive the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't the Abyss. I think she might have been dead long before then. The Persten we spoke to was an imposter.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: An imposter? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A Mahjarrat called Sliske was disguised as her.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I can't believe it. Persten and I had our differences, but this...

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I'd better share this news with the others. Thank you for bringing it to me.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Hey, [player name]. I heard about Persten. I can't believe it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, Felix. I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: I wish I could have met her... The real her I mean. I bet she'd have known how to properly close this rift.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: I guess it's just down to us though. Speaking of which, back to work I guess. Did you have any abyssal pearls you wanted to trade?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Hello, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. How are things here?

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Things aren't easy for the family, especially since they never got closure. They're getting through it, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I'm not sure if it will help them much, but I do have some news on that assassin.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He was actually a Mahjarrat in disguise. I got close to dealing with him, but he escaped.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: A Mahjarrat? I don't even know what one of those is... You say he escaped?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: For now, but I don't intend to let him keep eluding me forever.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: Well I wish you luck in your search, adventurer. Thank you for the update.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have something to tell you. It's about another dwarf.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Another dwarf? An Imcando Dwarf?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I encountered someone I believed to be a dwarf, but it was a lie. It was a Mahjarrat in disguise as someone called Ketla.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ketla...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You knew her?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I did, long ago. Is she...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's gone, Ramarno. I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I'm sorry, [player name], but let us not speak of this further. To learn of the death of another of my kin is no surprise, but it pains me all the same. I would prefer to focus my thoughts elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About Ketla...

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but the person I thought was Ketla... It wasn't her at all.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: What? But then... what happened to her?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's gone, Ramarno. I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I'm sorry, [player name], but let us not speak of this further. To learn of the death of another of my kin is no surprise, but it pains me all the same. I would prefer to focus my thoughts elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Good day Proselyte. Is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have news on that assassin who attacked Entrana.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Ah! Do tell, old bean.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did you know he was a Mahjarrat?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: A Mahjarrat! You must be pulling on my leg, what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: His name is Sliske. Have you heard of him?

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: Sliske, hmm? We have dossiers on many Mahjarrat, but I must confess, this is a new name to me. Poor show! I'll have to see what we have hiding in the archives.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: While your there, it might be worth looking up something called the Elder Horn. It's an artefact that Sliske has now claimed. One that has the ability to control minds.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: A mind controlling artefact you say? Sounds like I'll need to report this to the top brass. Can't see them being too thrilled about it. Thank you for the update, old bean.

lines.speaker: Sir Tiffy Cashien
lines.text: A mind controlling artefact you say? First Lucien and the Stone, and now this! The top brass will need to be made aware. Can't see them being too thrilled about it. Thank you for the update, old bean.
